diagrams.title: Remote User Authentication Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart diagram showing the complete remote user authentication process from user login request through successful authentication and resource access. The diagram illustrates the interaction between the remote user/client device, the network/communication channel, and the authentication server that validates credentials before granting access to protected resources.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental flow of remote user authentication, showing how credentials travel securely from a remote client to an authentication server and how access decisions are made. This helps understand the basic architecture and key components involved in securing remote access.

diagrams.title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Process
diagrams.description: A diagram illustrating the three authentication factors (Something You Know, Something You Have, Something You Are) and how they combine in Multi-Factor Authentication. The diagram shows separate verification paths for each factor and how they converge at the authentication decision point.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how Multi-Factor Authentication combines multiple independent authentication methods to create stronger security. This diagram helps learners understand the concept of layered security and why using multiple factor types provides better protection than single-factor authentication.

diagrams.title: Challenge-Response Authentication Protocol
diagrams.description: A sequence diagram showing the Challenge-Response authentication mechanism used in remote authentication. The diagram depicts the step-by-step exchange between client and server including the server issuing a random challenge, the client responding with a computed value, and the server validating the response.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Challenge-Response protocol which is fundamental to secure remote authentication. This diagram helps understand how systems defend against replay attacks and password interception by using dynamic challenges rather than transmitting static credentials.
